title: --cache-to causes compression_format from containers.conf to be ignored
body:
## Description

When using `podman build --cache-to`, the `compression_format` setting from `containers.conf` is ignored for both the cache layers AND the final image. Without `--cache-to`, the compression format is respected correctly.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a minimal `containers.conf` with zstd:chunked:

```ini
[engine]
compression_format="zstd:chunked"
```

2. Create a minimal Dockerfile:

```dockerfile
FROM alpine:latest
RUN echo "layer1" > /layer1.txt
RUN echo "layer2" > /layer2.txt
```

3. Build and push WITHOUT `--cache-to`:

```bash
podman build --no-cache --layers -t quay.io/jdanek/cache-compression-test:v8 .
podman push quay.io/jdanek/cache-compression-test:v8
crane manifest quay.io/jdanek/cache-compression-test:v8 | jq -r '.layers[].mediaType'
```

**Result**: `application/vnd.oci.image.layer.v1.tar+zstd` ✓ (correct)

4. Build and push WITH `--cache-to`:

```bash
podman system reset -f
podman build --no-cache --layers --cache-to quay.io/jdanek/cache-compression-test-cache-v6 -t quay.io/jdanek/cache-compression-test:v7 .
podman push quay.io/jdanek/cache-compression-test:v7
crane manifest quay.io/jdanek/cache-compression-test:v7 | jq -r '.layers[].mediaType'
```

**Result**: `application/vnd.oci.image.layer.v1.tar+gzip` ✗ (should be zstd)

The cache also uses gzip:

```bash
crane manifest quay.io/jdanek/cache-compression-test-cache-v6:077cd4b1a9a353fc2e41beb42b498264aef7287aed9675da9899238a4ddec6cc | jq -r '.layers[].mediaType'
```

**Result**: `application/vnd.oci.image.layer.v1.tar+gzip`

## Expected Behavior

Both cache and final image should use `zstd:chunked` compression as specified in `containers.conf`, regardless of whether `--cache-to` is used.

## Actual Behavior

| Scenario | Cache | Final Image |
|----------|-------|-------------|
| Build with `--layers` (no cache-to) | N/A | **zstd** ✓ |
| Build with `--layers --cache-to` | **gzip** | **gzip** |

The `--cache-to` flag causes the entire `compression_format` setting to be ignored.

## Environment

```
podman version 5.7.1
macOS with podman machine (Fedora CoreOS VM)
```

## containers.conf location

`/etc/containers/containers.conf` in the podman machine VM

## Related Issues

- #4613 - Buildah reuse gzip layers when "--compression-format zstd" assigned (fixed)
- https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/26088 - podman build removes layer compression when layering on compressed images

## Test Images

The test images are publicly available for verification:
- Cache: `quay.io/jdanek/cache-compression-test-cache-v6`
- Final (with cache-to, gzip): `quay.io/jdanek/cache-compression-test:v7`
- Final (without cache-to, zstd): `quay.io/jdanek/cache-compression-test:v8`

### storage.conf

```toml
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
runroot = "/run/containers/storage"
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"

[storage.options]
additionalimagestores = [
  "/usr/lib/containers/storage"
]

[storage.options.overlay]
mountopt = "nodev,metacopy=on"
```

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes - tested with podman 5.7.1 which includes buildah 1.42.2 (latest at time of testing).

### Additional environment details

- macOS host (darwin/arm64) running podman machine
- Podman machine running Fedora CoreOS 43 (linux/arm64)
- Podman installed via Homebrew on macOS
- containers.conf with `compression_format="zstd:chunked"` placed in `/etc/containers/containers.conf` inside the podman machine VM
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Let’s see what Dosu is capable of. I have an idea of where the issue lies.

<!-- Greeting -->
Hi @Honny1! I'm [Dosu](https://go.dosu.dev/dosubot) and I’m helping the buildah team.

<!-- Answer -->
This is a confirmed bug! There's an existing issue tracking this: [#6072 - cache compression algorithm](https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/6072#issuecomment-2762823832).

**Root Cause**: The [`pushCache()` function](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/c86654bf193f8d59d15d41458221ee4faaccbcc9/imagebuildah/stage_executor.go#L2341-L2357) uses the legacy `Compression` field (which only supports gzip/uncompressed) instead of the modern `CompressionFormat` field that supports zstd. Additionally, [`GenBuildOptions()` never reads `compression_format` from containers.conf](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/c86654bf193f8d59d15d41458221ee4faaccbcc9/pkg/cli/build.go#L230-233).

In contrast, regular `buildah push` [correctly sets `CompressionFormat`](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/c86654bf193f8d59d15d41458221ee4faaccbcc9/cmd/buildah/push.go#L227-L236) and respects containers.conf settings.

**Status**: The issue remains open with no fix available. Your only workaround currently is to avoid `--cache-to` or accept gzip compression for cache layers.

Your report here has great detail - it may be worth linking this issue to #6072 to help consolidate the bug reports.


<!-- Dosu Comment Footer -->
*To reply, just mention [@dosu](https://go.dosu.dev/dosubot?utm_source=github&utm_medium=bot-comment&utm_campaign=github-comment-footer-20260415&utm_content=reply-with-mention&utm_term=containers%2Fbuildah).*

---

[![Leave Feedback](https://img.shields.io/badge/Leave%20Feedback-555555?style=flat)](https://app.dosu.dev/response-feedback?utm_source=github&utm_medium=bot-comment&utm_campaign=github-comment-footer-20260415&utm_content=knowledge-infrastructure-feedback&utm_term=containers%2Fbuildah#message_id=21d6dce3-9c8c-420c-b94d-300ad5632a6e) [![Learn about buildah with Dosu](https://img.shields.io/badge/Learn%20about%20buildah%20with%20Dosu-2f7b3f?style=flat&logo=data%3Aimage%2Fsvg%2Bxml%3Bbase64%%%%2BPC9zdmc%2B)](https://github.dosu.com/containers/buildah?utm_source=github&utm_medium=bot-comment&utm_campaign=github-comment-footer-20260415&utm_content=knowledge-infrastructure-learn-repo&utm_term=containers%2Fbuildah) [![Add Dosu to your team](https://img.shields.io/badge/Add%20Dosu%20to%20your%20team-1f6feb?style=flat)](https://app.dosu.dev/signup?referrer=openSource&source=github-footer&utm_source=github&utm_medium=bot-comment&utm_campaign=github-comment-footer-20260415&utm_content=knowledge-infrastructure-add-team&utm_term=containers%2Fbuildah)

While Dosu provided some helpful information, if I hadn't spent enough time understanding the context and the code, I wouldn't have known what was going on.

To sum up the problem, there are two main parts:
- Compression Mismatch: `podman push` does not push the correct layer compression by default. This can be manually fixed using the `--force-compression` flag, which resolves the issue.
- Existing Blobs: The repository already contains layers pushed with `gzip`. When a push is performed after a build, Podman checks if those blobs already exist in the repository. If they do, it skips pushing the layer, meaning the "correct" compression version is never uploaded.

Possible Fixes:
1. Push layers to the cache using the correct compression format from the start.
2. Force Podman to honor the compression settings defined in containers.conf during the push process.

WDYT? @nalind  

Personally, I prefer the first option, but it basically means exposing new flags for the cache to set the compression type or sourcing that information directly from containers.conf.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
